sharedQueryShape: Extraction planning is the only BuildWorkOrders caller that ranks routes and repeatedly fans out from the settlement anchor or central depot.
The anchor and central depot currently share the same position, while extraction destinations are bounded by at most 96 resource sites.
Citizen selection shares work-order destinations but retains exact citizen origins, so route reuse is safe only for exact versioned endpoint keys.
The final frontier discards more than ninety-two percent of generated work orders, making exact pre-route dominance the smallest safe reduction surface.

architectureInput.selectedSmallestSlice: Omit a whole extraction resource class before route ranking only when the existing unclaimed frontier already contains K orders whose priorities are strictly greater than the class maximum priority.
architectureInput.proofBoundary: Because final ordering is priority descending then ordinal ID, K strictly higher-priority existing orders exclude every omitted class member from the retained K regardless of route distance, reachability, or later equal-priority ties.
requiredSafeguards: Remove active claimed orders before evaluating frontier occupancy.
Fall back if any active claimed ID collides with an eligible extraction ID, regardless of recorded order kind.
Use a strict greater-than cutoff; equality must materialize normally.
Preserve the virtual uncapped count and invoke final sorting/capping whenever the virtual count exceeds K.
Share the built-corridor priority bonus between the upper bound and exact order priority.
Restrict omission to capped exact_bounded mode; exhaustive_reference and uncapped modes remain independent references.
rejectedHigherRiskAlternatives: multi-target search that could change authoritative A-star tie paths
suffix or reverse-route reuse without a proven asymmetric-cost contract
using lower-bound fields as exact routes or scores

differentialCorrectness: Strict, equality, underfilled, corridor, claimed-ID, duplicate-ID, virtual-count, ordering, unreachable, and negative-cache boundaries must preserve the exhaustive result.
All four shipped scenarios must match assignments, worker state, events, harvest application, resources, produced and consumed totals, depot state, and snapshots for 300 ticks.
Routes, float results, deterministic ties, exact endpoints, unreachable behavior, and navigation-version invalidation remain authoritative and unchanged.
cleanExportRelease: Implementation must be committed and source clean before collection.
Standalone forced invalidation, all 14 canonical pairs, and a separate three-reference confirmation must pass their contracts.
All canonical manifest artifact hashes must verify.
performanceGate: Every 16-citizen reference trial p95 must be at or below 50 milliseconds and every maximum at or below 250 milliseconds.
Both 1000-tick soak p95 values must be at or below 50 milliseconds and maxima at or below 250 milliseconds.
Forced commit-to-first-correct-lookup must be at or below 250 milliseconds.
W1-06 remains blocked if any hard safety limit fails; Week 2 additionally remains blocked until W1-06 is complete.

implementation.priorityUpperBound: base priority plus the existing 40-point built-corridor bonus
implementation.omissionRule: At least K active unclaimed existing priorities are strictly greater than the resource-class upper bound, no active claimed ID collides, and the class has desired units plus eligible sites.
implementation.virtualCount: Omitted count is min(desiredUnits, eligibleSiteCount) and remains included in WorkOrdersGeneratedUncapped.
unchangedPaths: claimed-ID collision fallback including legacy kind mismatches
authoritative versioned A-star, positive and negative cache semantics, exact endpoints, unreachable behavior, and deterministic ordering

observability.diagnostic: ExtractionOrdersOmitted is a derived per-tick simulation diagnostic only.

residualCharacterization.interpretation: The slice cuts total route lookups by 20.157 percent and the 16-citizen reference lookup count by 8.902 percent while preserving the virtual uncapped frontier. Aggregate exact misses remain effectively flat because omitted work is predominantly repeated positive-cache traffic and natural-warm cache population shifts some later misses. The optimization therefore earns the safety decision from exact frontier omission and measured Release timings, not from a claim that every matrix miss count fell.

resolvedFindings: Restored legacy route, cache, append, and final-removal behavior for claimed extraction candidates on the fallback path.
Added controlled call-site coverage rather than relying only on detached planning helpers.
Corrected the differential assertion to count general misses as total misses minus selector misses.

decision.remainingWeek2Blocker: W1-06 resource authority and snapshot/schema work has not yet been implemented or validated.
decision.stressCharacterization: The non-gating 24-citizen case remains above its 50 millisecond p95 target at 129.2682 milliseconds.
decision.next: Merge W1-05f, then begin W1-06 as the next unblocked Week 1 work item. Keep Week 2 feature expansion blocked until W1-06 and its hard correctness, persistence, and validation gates pass.
